question: can section 6.5 asymmetric allocation give the expert path the rate the pilot says it needs, without breaking the one-bit law
reading: routed experts are 0.975 of the weight, so the budget cannot be moved to them: even spending nothing at all on attention, embeddings, the head, the shared expert and the dense MLPs leaves the expert path 1.0186 bits under the one-bit law, against a measured requirement of 1.296924170790343 to 1.4607836072207667. Asymmetric allocation is the right instinct and the wrong lever here: there is no other role large enough to donate from.

scenarios.assumption: everything else free, the absolute bound
everything else at a quarter bit
everything else at half a bit
everything else free, the absolute bound
everything else at a quarter bit
everything else at half a bit

what_this_does_not_close: this bounds product quantization at a rate, not the expert path in general. A representation that reaches the trajectory floor at a lower rate is untested and is exactly what sections 6.1 to 6.3 propose.
